body.title: TWM: goal-relevance-weighted TTL decay
body.result: twm_relevance_score() + twm_apply_goal_decay() on cortex. Fires on goal shift + heartbeat. IGOR_TWM_GOAL_DECAY_PENALTY configurable. 18 tests. Session 2026-04-02a.
body.description:
TWM currently decays entries by time only (fixed TTL). Items unrelated to the active goal should decay faster; items directly relevant should hold longer.

Fix: at each decay pass, compute a relevance score for each TWM entry against the current ACTIVE_GOAL (from T-twm-goal-slot). Adjust effective decay rate by relevance.

Algorithm:
  relevance = word_graph_bigram_overlap(entry_content, active_goal_text)  # 0.0–1.0, no inference
  decay_multiplier = 1.0 + (1.0 - relevance) * GOAL_DECAY_PENALTY  # default PENALTY=2.0
  effective_ttl = base_ttl / decay_multiplier
  # low relevance (0.0) → decays 3× faster; high relevance (1.0) → decays at base rate

Implementation:
1. Add relevance_score field to TWM observation (computed at insert + recomputed on goal change)
2. In NE daemon decay pass (or TWM.tick()): apply goal-weighted multiplier
3. On ACTIVE_GOAL change: re-score all existing TWM entries immediately (goal shift flushes stale context fast)
4. ACTIVE_GOAL entry itself is exempt from relevance decay (it IS the goal)
5. Configurable: IGOR_TWM_GOAL_DECAY_PENALTY env var (default 2.0, range 1.0–5.0)

Word graph overlap: count shared bigrams between entry tokens and goal tokens, normalize by entry length. O(n) per entry, cheap enough for TWM size (~7-50 items).

Prerequisite: T-twm-goal-slot.
Design refs: Gemini threading analysis (RL_gemini_threading_20260401), temporal gradient primitive (project_temporal_gradient_primitive.md).
